title: Twin Studies and What They Tell Us About Tinnitus Risk
phrases: "Every resistance is information. The question is whether you're willing to read it."
"At a certain depth of inquiry, the distinction between psychology and philosophy dissolves entirely."
"What we call 'stuck' is usually the body doing exactly what it was designed to do under conditions that no longer exist."
interjections: And this is the part nobody talks about.
researchers: Josef Rauschecker (Georgetown neuroscientist, tinnitus brain mapping)
Richard Davidson (neuroscience of meditation, emotional styles)
existingBody:
Three weeks in. The fridge hums. Nothing has changed except everything.

title: Inflammation and Its Hidden Role in Tinnitus
phrases: "There's a meaningful difference between self-improvement and self-understanding. One adds. The other reveals."
"Consciousness doesn't arrive. It's what's left when everything else quiets down."
"The self you're trying to improve is the same self doing the improving. Notice the circularity."
"The gap between stimulus and response is where your entire life lives."
"What we call 'the present moment' is not a place you go. It's the only place you've ever been."
interjections: Think about that for a second.
Let that land for a second.
researchers: Stephen Porges (polyvagal theory, nervous system regulation)
existingBody:
I've sat across from someone saying exactly what you're feeling right now.

title: The Blood-Labyrinth Barrier and Inner Ear Protection
phrases: "You don't arrive at peace. You stop walking away from it."
"The body remembers what the mind would prefer to file away."
"Reading about meditation is to meditation what reading the menu is to eating."
"The wellness industry sells solutions to problems it helps you believe you have."
interjections: Stick with this for a moment.
Think about that for a second.
researchers: Bessel van der Kolk (body keeps the score, trauma and the body)
existingBody:
Alan Watts's work illuminates the path.

title: How Cardiovascular Health Affects Tinnitus Severity

"The paradox of acceptance is that nothing changes until you stop demanding that it does."
"Stop pathologizing normal human suffering. Not everything requires a diagnosis."

existingBody:

title: Pulsatile Tinnitus and Vascular Abnormalities

"Not every insight requires action. Some just need to be witnessed."
"Sit with it long enough and even the worst feeling reveals its edges."
